license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7uxhnxn
title: General Immunohistochemical Protocol for formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue sections
author: Tereza Blassova

steps_raw.instruction: Deparaffinize slides in 3 changes of xylene, 5 min each. 00:05:00
Drain off the blocking buffer from the slides.
Block endogenous peroxidase activity by incubating sections in Peroxidase-Blocking solution (Dako) at room temperature for 10 min to block endogenous peroxidase activity. 00:10:00
Rinse in 300ml of PBS for 3 changes, 5 min each. 00:05:00

Optional: Perform antigen retrieval to unmask the antigenic epitope. The most commonly used antigen retrieval is a citrate buffer method. Arrange the slides in a staining container. Pour 300ml of 10mM citrate buffer, pH 6.0 into the staining container and incubate it at 95-100°C for 10 min (optimal incubation time should be determined by user). Remove the staining container to room temperature and allow the slides to cool for 20 min. We used different ways depend to used primary antibody: - Heat-induced epitope retrieval in Epitope Retrieval Solution pH 9 X pH 6 (Novocastra Leica, Leica Biosystems GmbH, Nussloch, Germany); 20 minutes - Proteinase K (Dako) - 10 min.
Rinse slides in 300ml PBS for 3 changes, 5 min each. 00:05:00
Optional: Add 200µl blocking buffer (e.g. 10% goat serum in PBS) onto the sections of the slides and incubate in a humidified chamber at room temperature for 1 hour. 00:30:00

Apply 200µl appropriately diluted primary antibody (in antibody dilution buffer) to the sections on the slides and incubate in a humidified chamber at room temperature overnight. 16:00:00, overnight 4 °C

Apply 200µl appropriately diluted secondary antibody (using the antibody dilution buffer) to the sections on the slides and incubate in a humidified chamber at room temperature for 30 min. We used: N-Histofine ® Simple Stain MAX PO (NICHIREI CORPORATION INC.) Apply 2 drops of Simple Stain MAX PO (MULTI) to each slide so as to provide a complete cover of the sections. Incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Wash slides in 300ml PBS for 3 changes, 5 min each. 00:05:00

Apply 150 µl DAB Liquid solution (Dako) to the sections on the slides to reveal the color of antibody staining. Allow the color development for < 5 min until the desired color intensity is reached. (Caution: DAB is a suspect carcinogen. Handle with care. Wear gloves, lab coat and eye protection.)
Rinse slides in destil water, 5 min each. 00:05:00
Optional: Counterstain slides by immersing sides in Hematoxylin (e.g. Gill's Hematoxylin) for 1-2 min. 00:02:00
Rinse the slides in running tap water for > 15 min. 00:15:00
Clear the tissue slides in 3 changes of xylene and coverslip using mounting solution. The mounted slides can be stored at room temperature permanently.
Observe the color of the antibody staining in the tissue sections under microscopy.
Wash the slides in 300ml PBS for 3 changes 5 min each. 00:05:00
Dehydrate the tissue slides through 4 changes of alcohol (95%, 95%, 100% and 100%), 5 min each. 00:05:00
Transfer slides to 100% alcohol, for 2 changes, 3 min each, and then transfer once through 95%, 70% and 50% alcohols respectively for 3 min each. 00:05:00
